claim: Hypertension can be accurately diagnosed with ambulatory blood pressure monitoring.

result.claim: Hypertension can be accurately diagnosed with ambulatory blood pressure monitoring.
result.reasoning:
The claim that hypertension can be accurately diagnosed with ambulatory blood pressure monitoring (ABPM) is strongly supported by the evidence. Multiple sources identify ABPM as a diagnostic method for hypertension, with some even referring to it as the "gold standard." For instance, Paper ID 65484589 (Credibility: 3.9/5, RCT) explicitly states that "24-hour ambulatory blood pressure monitoring (ABPM) is a diagnostic method for hypertension" (prop_87319) and its abstract (chunk_4103) calls it the "gold standard diagnostic method."

Furthermore, ABPM is highlighted for its ability to differentiate between sustained hypertension and white coat hypertension, which is crucial for accurate diagnosis and management (prop_87354, chunk_4109 from Paper ID 40836717, Credibility: 1.1/5, observational). A review paper (Paper ID W3047559810, Credibility: 3.0/5) emphasizes that "Out-of-office blood pressure measurement is essential in diagnosing and managing hypertension" (prop_87329, chunk_4105), which includes ABPM. Another observational study (Paper ID 023562d0b199f5808444b343a06901160312dca8, Credibility: 1.8/5) reported that a large percentage of patients were diagnosed with hypertension based on 24-hour ABPM criteria (prop_86917). Practice guidelines (Paper ID W2024956494, Credibility: 2.0/5, review) also provide clinical indications for out-of-office BP measurement for diagnostic purposes and define cut-off values for hypertension using ambulatory BP (chunk_4119). While some papers discuss other monitoring methods or their limitations, the overall evidence consistently points to ABPM as an accurate and valuable tool for hypertension diagnosis.

**Evidence Note:** 65484589, W3047559810, 023562d0b199f5808444b343a06901160312dca8, 40836717, W2024956494

evidence.text: Office BP measurement is currently the ‘gold standard’ for screening, diagnosis and management of hypertension.
evidence.source: 2013 Practice guidelines for the management of arterial hypertension of the European Society of Hypertension (ESH) and the European Society of Cardiology (ESC)

evidence.text: The 1-week averaged home SBP at the cut-off point of 141.0 mmHg provided a sensitivity of 85.7% in diagnosing systolic ambulatory hypertension.
evidence.source: #3505 DIAGNOSING HYPERTENSION IN HEMODIALYSIS PATIENTS USING HOME VERSUS ROUTINE PERIDIALYTIC BP MEASUREMENTS

evidence.text: Remission of hypertension was present in 22 of 48 (45.8%) patients randomized to gastric bypass considering 24-hour ambulatory blood pressure monitoring.
evidence.source: Effects of Bariatric Surgery in Obese Patients With Hypertension

evidence.text: Wearable blood pressure monitors allow frequent blood pressure measurements.
evidence.source: Management of Hypertension in the Digital Era

evidence.text: Blood pressure should be monitored in patients on antihypertensive therapy.
evidence.source: The Role of Omega-3 and Omega-6 Polyunsaturated Fatty Acid Supplementation in Human Health.

evidence.text: The study highlights a relation between 24-hour MAP and systolic/diastolic BP measurements in diagnosing hypertension via 24-hour ABPM.
evidence.source: Relationship of 24-Hour Mean Arterial Pressure with Systolic and Diastolic Blood Pressure in Hypertension: Insights from Ambulatory Blood Pressure Monitoring

evidence.text: Blood pressure monitoring is necessary in patients on antihypertensive therapy.
evidence.source: The Role of Omega-3 and Omega-6 Polyunsaturated Fatty Acid Supplementation in Human Health.

evidence.text: The study will compare a new method with office blood pressure monitoring (OBPM) and home blood pressure monitoring (HBPM).
evidence.source: Effectiveness of a new one-hour blood pressure monitoring method to diagnose hypertension: a diagnostic accuracy clinical trial protocol

evidence.text: The study aims to study the validity of a new method of diagnosing hypertension consisting of monitoring blood pressure (BP) for 1 hour.
evidence.source: Effectiveness of a new one-hour blood pressure monitoring method to diagnose hypertension: a diagnostic accuracy clinical trial protocol

evidence.text: HBPM for 1 week provides greater accuracy than 2-week averaged routine pre- and postdialysis BP recordings in the diagnosis of hypertension in patients on hemodialysis.
evidence.source: #3505 DIAGNOSING HYPERTENSION IN HEMODIALYSIS PATIENTS USING HOME VERSUS ROUTINE PERIDIALYTIC BP MEASUREMENTS

evidence.text: The 1-week averaged home SBP at the cut-off point of 141.0 mmHg provided a specificity of 92.9% in diagnosing systolic ambulatory hypertension.
evidence.source: #3505 DIAGNOSING HYPERTENSION IN HEMODIALYSIS PATIENTS USING HOME VERSUS ROUTINE PERIDIALYTIC BP MEASUREMENTS

evidence.text: 24-hour ambulatory blood pressure monitoring (ABPM) is a diagnostic method for hypertension.
evidence.source: Effectiveness of a new one-hour blood pressure monitoring method to diagnose hypertension: a diagnostic accuracy clinical trial protocol

evidence.text: Wearable devices are expected to change the quality of detection and management of hypertension.
evidence.source: Management of Hypertension in the Digital Era

evidence.text: Ambulatory blood pressure monitoring (ABPM) helps differentiate sustained hypertension (SH) from white coat hypertension (WCH).
evidence.source: White Coat Hypertension and Its Predictors in Newly Diagnosed Hypertensive Patients Attending a Tertiary Care Center.

evidence.text: Physical examination aims to establish or verify the diagnosis of hypertension.
evidence.source: 2013 Practice guidelines for the management of arterial hypertension of the European Society of Hypertension (ESH) and the European Society of Cardiology (ESC)

evidence.text: Out-of-office blood pressure measurement is essential in diagnosing and managing hypertension.

evidence.text: Remission of hypertension was present in 25 of 49 (51%) patients randomized to gastric bypass considering office blood pressure monitoring.

evidence.text: Sustained hypertension (SH) was diagnosed in 143 out of 196 patients.
evidence.source: White Coat Hypertension and Its Predictors in Newly Diagnosed Hypertensive Patients Attending a Tertiary Care Center.

evidence.text: The study examined whether blood pressure monitoring was received.

evidence.text: 409 patients (76.9%) were diagnosed with hypertension based on 24-hour ambulatory systolic/diastolic BP criteria.
